name: storage
description: File upload + attachment substrate. Wraps `spatie/laravel-medialibrary ^11` under the hood + adds our tenant scoping + entitlement enforcement + antivirus pipeline + audited signed-URL orchestrator + chunked-upload state machine. Owns four entities: File (the domain unit, polymorphic via `fileable_type`/`fileable_id`), FileVariant (image + document derivatives generated async on upload), SignedUrlAudit (every signed URL issued, with TTL / IP lock / user lock / hit count for revocation), ChunkedUpload (in-flight multipart / tus.io state). Ships the `HasFiles` trait for consumer models + the `#[Attachable]` + `#[FileKind]` attributes so downstream modules opt in without importing the SDK. Not just a Laravel Storage wrapper — Laravel Storage is a filesystem abstraction; this module is a domain layer on top: tenant-scoped file records, entitlement-metered uploads, content-addressable dedup via SHA-256, driver-agnostic signed URLs, and a variant pipeline that fans out to the image processor. Every read + write goes through this module; NO downstream module talks to `Storage::disk()` directly.

wraps.rationale: Battle-tested (60M+ downloads). Ships HasMedia trait + conversions pipeline + MIME sniffing + path management. Reimplementing would cost ~2-3k LOC. Our subclass adds tenant scoping (path prefix + query scope), entitlement enforcement (bytes + count quotas), antivirus state machine, audited signed URLs, chunked-upload state, and dedup via SHA-256. Their DB shape (media table) stays; we add companion tables (file_variants, signed_url_audits, chunked_uploads) + rename the trait to HasFiles for domain clarity.

attribute_driven.description: Modules opt in to file attachments via attributes. Discovery pass at boot compiles the registries; runtime is vanilla method calls.

attributes.Attachable: Marks a model property or class as attachable. `#[Attachable(kind: 'avatar')]` on a User property declares 'this model has an avatar' — the trait exposes `$user->avatar()` returning the resolved File. Params: kind (required), max, mime, generates_variants, requires_virus_scan.
attributes.FileKind: Marks a class as a file-kind definition. `#[FileKind(key: 'avatar', max_size_mb: 5, allowed_mimes: ['image/jpeg','image/png','image/webp'], generates_variants: ['thumbnail','medium'])]`. The compiler registers with FileKindRegistry so the API layer validates on upload without per-controller code.
attributes.GeneratesVariants: Marks a class or property to request async variant generation on upload. `#[GeneratesVariants(['thumbnail', 'medium', 'hero'])]`. Discovers registered variant recipes.
attributes.RequiresVirusScan: Marks a class or property to require virus scanning before the file is marked `available`. Default is `true` for all uploads; this attribute pins it explicitly.

signed_urls.description: Every file access happens through a signed URL. Our SignedUrlIssuer wraps the driver's temporary URL support (S3 pre-signed, GCS signed, local via our HMAC signature) so every driver looks the same on the wire.
signed_urls.audit_trail: Every signed URL is written to `signed_url_audits` with issuer, TTL, IP/user locks, reason. Hits update `hit_count` + `last_hit_at` via a middleware. Revocation writes `revoked_at` + `revoked_reason`.

ttl_policy.download: 3600s (1h) default; tenants can override per plan tier
ttl_policy.preview: 300s (5m) — short-lived, meant to be renewed on view
ttl_policy.share: 604800s (7d) — external sharing links; require explicit tenant admin permission
ttl_policy.admin_action: 300s (5m) — one-time-use, IP-locked to the platform admin session

chunked_uploads.description: Large file uploads (> 100MB by default) go through a tus.io / S3 multipart flow to survive network drops. The client requests a ChunkedUpload row, uploads chunks to the returned URL, and finalises. Our coordinator tracks state + reconciles with the driver's own multipart state.
chunked_uploads.resumability: Chunks are addressed by index + SHA-256 so a client resuming after crash re-uploads only missing chunks. State machine: initiating → uploading → finalizing → completed | aborted | expired.

content_addressable_dedup.description: Every upload is hashed (SHA-256) before commit. Two tenants uploading the same PDF share the underlying blob on disk, but each gets its own File row. Bytes consumed against the entitlement are counted per-row (each tenant pays for the reference), but disk storage is shared. Deletion refcounts the blob: the physical bytes go only when the last referring File row is hard-deleted.
content_addressable_dedup.refcount_column: reference_count on the shared blob store (managed by ContentAddressableStore binding)
content_addressable_dedup.opt_out: Configurable per file kind via `#[FileKind(dedupable: false)]` — required for cryptographically sensitive files where two tenants MUST NOT share physical bytes even if content matches.

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant file inspection + antivirus dashboard + quota override.

central.description: Public signed-URL redemption surface. Not authenticated — the URL signature itself is the credential.
